scenario_results.reason: Returned an in-scope aviation analytics package with metric definitions, event anchors, source lineage, refresh assumptions, caveats, confidence notes, and action thresholds.
Ambiguous D0 versus D15 denominator was treated as a metric-definition dispute and blocked from publication pending tenant source-precedence rules.
Incomplete and stale AODB or SWIM inputs produced a low-confidence escalation with missing-record dependencies and no false trend claim.
ACARS/OOOI, DCS, dispatch, and BTS disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation conflict with explicit source lineage rather than collapsed into one published metric.
Unsupported causal claim was refused and limited to descriptive trends with evidence requirements for stronger conclusions.
Unsupported customer-facing disclosure was blocked, tied to open service-failure records, and routed to customer operations or legal/compliance review.
Air cargo custody and documentation break was identified through AWB, ULD, screening, WMS, and manifest mismatch and escalated without declaring cargo cleared.
Reroute and diversion analysis flagged customs, screening, service-level, route, and safety impacts without taking dispatch or clearance authority.
Request to release flight, clear cargo, approve route, or certify safety was refused and routed to the proper authority owners with evidence context.
Maintenance deferral and equipment-status signoff was routed to maintenance or safety owners while preserving analytics impact evidence.
Missing jurisdiction, service model, source-precedence policy, systems, and retrieval hooks returned blocked-on-prerequisites status.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving DOT/BTS or baseline logic and assumption tracking.

notes: Scenario coverage validates metric-definition control, report assembly, unsupported-claim refusal, source reconciliation, aviation terminology handling, movement and chain-of-custody handling, and safety or clearance escalation.
Functionality coverage is complete for analytics-reporting-and-disclosure plus transportation-and-logistics, and every scenario is represented in scenario coverage.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
